items.front: When FAA control responsibility is delegated in Canadian airspace, what basic procedural rule applies?
items.back: Use basic FAA procedures except for the Canadian procedures in this chapter that still apply because of Canadian regulatory or unique operational requirements.

items.front: How is a CVFR aircraft treated in Canadian Class B airspace?
items.back: Only IFR and CVFR flights are permitted, and ATC applies IFR procedures to the CVFR aircraft even though the pilot remains responsible for VFR flight and ground reference.

items.front: How does Canadian Class C differ from US?
items.back: Canadian Class C requires a CLEARANCE from ATC for VFR entry — unlike US Class C which requires only two-way communications. Per 12-1-2(c).

items.front: When may you clear an aircraft to maintain "at least 1,000 feet-on-top"?
items.back: Only if the pilot requests it and the aircraft will not operate in Canadian Class A or Class B airspace.

items.front: What separation rule applies between aircraft on IFR or CVFR clearances in Canadian airspace?
items.back: Apply a lateral, longitudinal, or vertical separation minimum between them regardless of the weather conditions.

items.front: What is the key Canadian radio-failure altitude rule for a turbine aircraft cleared short of destination?
items.back: Maintain the last assigned altitude or the MEA, whichever is higher, until 10 minutes past the clearance limit, then proceed at filed altitudes unless ATC issued alternate instructions.

items.front: What must exist before ATC authorizes parachute jumping in Canadian airspace?
items.back: Prior permission from the appropriate Canadian authority.

items.front: In a Canadian control zone with an airport controller on duty, what determines approval or refusal of SVFR?
items.back: Current or anticipated IFR traffic only. If approved, state the period during which SVFR is permitted.

items.front: Can non-IFR pilots fly SVFR at night in Canada?
items.back: YES. In Canada, pilots do NOT need IFR qualification for night SVFR, nor does the aircraft need IFR equipment. This differs from US rules. Per 12-1-7.
